//: Copy to agenthook.config.json in your project dir (or run `agenthook init`).
//secrets: Secrets are ${ENV} refs, resolved from the shell or a .env beside this file,
//secrets2: so this file stays shareable. State lives centrally in ~/.agenthook/<name>.
name: myproject
//fullAuto: false (safe default) = agents prompt for permission. true = --dangerously-skip-permissions: a verified webhook runs UNSANDBOXED code on the host. Opt in only on a trusted host or in a container. See docs/architecture.md#security-posture.

tracker.//: type selects the adapter; the rest is that tracker's config.
tracker.//github: GitHub Issues instead: { type:'github', repository:'owner/name', token:'${GITHUB_TOKEN}' } — steps bind labels (sourceLabel/successLabel/...). See docs/github-setup.md.
tracker.//github-projects: GitHub Projects v2 instead: { type:'github-projects', project:'owner/number', token:'${GITHUB_TOKEN}' } — steps bind board Status options (sourceStatus/successStatus/..., like Jira). ORG webhook + STABLE ingress required. See docs/github-projects-setup.md.
tracker.//pipeline: The execution model (required). A task ENTERING a step's sourceSectionGid fires
tracker.//pipeline2: that step. On exit the agent writes a VERDICT and the receiver moves the task:
tracker.//pipeline3: advance -> successSectionGid (= the next step's source, so the move triggers it);
tracker.//pipeline4: fail -> failureSectionGid (non-zero exit, or the agent says so); hold -> holdSectionGid
tracker.//pipeline5: (parked for a human answer); changes -> a prior step's source (the rework loop, capped
tracker.//pipeline6: by maxAttempts, default 3). createsWorktree makes the receiver own a git worktree
tracker.//pipeline7: shared across the task's steps; drainWorktree removes it; manual = no agent (entry
tracker.//pipeline8: only runs system actions). Each step's instructionsFile is its standing prompt.
tracker.//pipeline9: Forward motion is all event-driven; `agenthook reconcile` is the only (explicit) poll.

pipeline.//holdSectionGid: optional: where a `hold` parks a task waiting on a human answer

pipeline.//maxAttempts: optional: cap on runs per task before a `changes` loop into this step fails (default 3)

pipeline.//changes: a review `changes` verdict bounces back to the previous step (code) on the same branch/PR

ingress.//: How the receiver is reachable. ngrok (managed tunnel) | manual/hosted (static URL).
ingress.//domain: optional reserved domain for a stable URL (omit = ephemeral)
ingress.//manual: For a hosted URL instead: { type: hosted, url: https://hook.you.com }
